digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design reference and skill integration
propositions: Create a canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast default, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals, with the scale-to-the-bug principle explicit.

Ship byte-identical real-file copies of test-design.md in the rebar-debug, rebar-implement, and rebar-brainstorm skill directories using the same-directory companion-file pattern, avoiding symlinks that break on Windows checkouts and zip archives.

Add a gating unit test tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py that asserts each skill copy is byte-identical to the canonical file and self-verifies its detection by asserting a perturbed copy diverges.

Integrate the shared file into rebar-debug: retitle Step 4 to RED-oracle design, defer the LLM-surface paragraph to the shared quantified-eval standard, make Step 5's mutation check defect-seeded, and add the frozen contract card as a fourth fixer handoff item without exposing held-out test code.
Qualify llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md's Phase 2 (Repair) bullet to cite test-design.md's quantified-eval requirements so the two files cannot disagree.

Wire rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b and rebar-brainstorm's plan-review-gate section to reference the shared file, and document the canonical-plus-copies drift convention with the sync one-liner in README.md.
